name: Behalf.bot - Loom Vision
description: Process a Loom share URL into multimodal context: downloads the video, samples key frames at 1 frame per 5 seconds, and pairs them with the auto-generated transcript. Lets Claude see what's happening on screen during a walkthrough, code review, or bug repro - not just what was said. Token-cheap visual context for any Loom link.

enabled.description: Top-level toggle. When false the skill won't trigger.

frame_interval_seconds.description: How often to sample a frame from the source video. Lower = more visual detail but more frames to process. 5s is a sensible default for code walkthroughs and demos.

frame_max_width_px.description: Max width in pixels for sampled frames. 1280 is enough for code legibility on most screens.

frame_quality.description: ffmpeg JPEG quality (lower = better quality, 1-31 scale). 3 is a good balance of legibility vs. file size for code screenshots.

output_root.description: Directory where per-video output folders are written. Each Loom video gets a subdir named loom-<video_id> containing video.mp4, transcript.vtt, transcript.txt, transcript.json, and frame_NNN.jpg files.

activation._note: Skill is invoked on-demand when the user pastes a Loom URL or asks the agent to process Loom video content. No scheduled jobs.

system.name: node
system._explanation: Runs the transcript JSON to VTT/plaintext conversion embedded in process-loom.sh. Also required transitively by loom-dl (it is an npm CLI), so a working loom-dl implies node is present. Listed explicitly so a chassis that ships loom-dl as a prebuilt binary still installs node.

system.name: loom-dl
system._explanation: loom-dl is a small Node CLI that handles the Loom share URL to mp4 + transcript download. Not on Homebrew, ships via npm. Verified against loom-dl 1.1.1, which writes the transcript as <basename>.transcript.json (schemaVersion 1.1.x).

system.name: ffmpeg
system._explanation: Frame sampling, plus ffprobe (bundled) to read video duration for bounding the final transcript cue. Any recent ffmpeg works.

_chassis_v1_status._explanation: Self-contained plugin with two small system deps. No network state, no per-installer config beyond the optional knobs above. Safe to enable by default.
